page: Amazon Web Services r4.8xlarge is a memory-optimized fourth-generation instance type. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 244.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 7.62 GB per core. The CPU includes a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested servers. Similarly, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are positioned in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all ranking in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average performance. Large-model LLM inference is a bottleneck, with 70B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r4.8xlarge offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. Because it lacks local NVMe storage, storage-heavy operations must rely on network-attached storage. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that benefit from a large memory footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services r4.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 244.0 GB of system memory, a 45 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently score in the average range. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high single-threaded speeds. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r4.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 244.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 CPU. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core speeds.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring 244 GB of RAM and 32 vCPUs for memory-intensive, multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
